note: Chapter image sizing was standardized on zoom so image elements and chapter settings use the same geometry terminology.
The back cover now chooses an explicit shared image asset instead of inheriting the front cover image through a boolean flag.
Shared cover metadata sizing was replaced by per-element size controls for author, series, and publisher.
Generated cover previews now live in the shared file registry and are referenced by id instead of embedding raw image data directly on the book.
System-managed book identity now lives under meta so authored content is easier to hash independently from runtime metadata.
Revision tracking is now grouped under meta with the rest of the non-authored bookkeeping fields.
Subrevision tracking is now grouped under meta with the rest of the non-authored bookkeeping fields.
Copy and import lineage now lives under meta so content data stays distinct from system history.
Creation timestamps are now grouped under meta with other system-managed dates.
Edit timestamps are now grouped under meta with other system-managed dates.
Export timestamps are now grouped under meta with other system-managed dates.
Import timestamps are now grouped under meta with other system-managed dates.
The generated cover preview image reference now lives under meta and uses the clearer coverPreviewImageId name.
Derived page counts are now grouped under meta because they are generated by the app rather than authored by the user.
The page count field no longer uses an underscore prefix.

Subchapter indentation is now controlled entirely by toc.indentStep. A value of 0 means no indentation, so the old checkbox is no longer needed.
